programmes.show_description: A head-on car smash in Cumbria leaves Nick's life hanging in the balance, and the heli-medics battle extreme weather to get to a man trapped under his JCB.
Brian, who's 74, is taken to King's by ambulance after falling down some stairs. His partner Margaret discusses their life together and the challenges of his severe dementia.
On the last day in Barcelona, Stuart slides into action with a trip to an inflatable water park. Is it enough to bring balmy bliss, or will a frosty dinner put everyone off? (Ep15/20)
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Middlesbrough. Glamorous sales assistant Gail hosts a night of gourmet grub and gets the glitter, sparklers and hair extensions out to give her guests a masterclass in beauty. (Ep1/5)
Middlesbrough. Goalkeeping coach Dan hosts a James Bond evening, with 007-inspired food, a villain and... a penalty shoot-out. But some of his guests don't bond with each other. (Ep2/5)
Middlesbrough. Publican Gary hosts a 1980s night, flambéing his steaks at the table. There's tension between the guests, but Gary has a trick up his sleeve: a magic display. (Ep3/5)
Middlesbrough. Vintage shop owner Jane serves up a retro menu and a clothes competition, on a lively night with yet more drama between the diners. (Ep4/5)
Middlesbrough. For the final night, art enthusiast Julie serves an Indian menu. But her food gets mixed reviews, and there are shocks, revelations and some harsh home truths. (Ep5/5)
Alison and Lee are moving to Almería's countryside. They need room for their six children and three grandkids to visit, and a garden for their dogs. Can Ben Hillman deliver for their £150k?
Brian and Lorna are after the perfect holiday home on Spain's Almería coast, with plenty of outdoor space for their pet dog Angus. Lee Juggurnauth shows them what their £150k can buy.
Georgina and Robert dream of owning a holiday home on the Caribbean island of St Lucia, where Georgina's family are from. Jean Johansson shows them what their £200k budget can buy.
Can Ben Hillman help professional dog sitter Sally find her perfect holiday home in Andalucia's white-washed villages, for her budget of £65,000?

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Abi and her daughter Maddy's Victorian cottage in Surrey isn't working for them. Will Phil's fabulous houses win them over, or could Kirstie's renovation clinch it?
In Bournemouth, Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two single parents who want to bring their families together, and a family who are stuck in a rental rut.
...Renovations: Yohance is turning a cavernous, dark office on a commercial estate in London into his first home. Can he transform a sterile space into a stylish house?
The competition begins at Derwent House Apartments in Scarborough, where hosts Claire and her daughter-in-law Jessica want to change perceptions of the seaside town.
At Burton Constable Holiday Park near Hull, park wardens Gemma and Mark are hoping their luxurious lodges will wow the B&Bers. But there's a fly in the ointment for one pair of guests.
Pro chef Stephen hosts the week's third visit at The Crossings Inn in Cumbria. His ethos is no-nonsense accommodation and gorgeous grub, so eggs-pectations are sky high at breakfast time.
The week's last visit is to Jane's lovingly restored Plas Maenan Country House in Wales' Conwy Valley. But the restoration doesn't meet everyone's standards with patchy paintwork a problem.
It's payment day, and there's a packed agenda of cleanliness concerns and scrambled scores as the B&Bers air their grievances before the winner is announced.
Brand new series: The series with unprecedented access to the Royal Melbourne Hospital returns. Doctors fear surfer Andre will never walk again after he crashed into a sandbank.
Linden, who's 15, is at King's after cutting his leg backflipping off a shed onto a trampoline. And Antony - who has smoked for 40 years - has lost all feeling in his left leg.
Builder Jamie is rushed in to A&E after falling from the roof of a three-storey building. Eighty-year-old Daphne has dislocated her replacement hip while putting on her socks.
Brand new series: The series with unprecedented access to the Royal Melbourne Hospital returns. Doctors fear surfer Andre will never walk again after he crashed into a sandbank.
Linden, who's 15, is at King's after cutting his leg backflipping off a shed onto a trampoline. And Antony - who has smoked for 40 years - has lost all feeling in his left leg.
Builder Jamie is rushed in to A&E after falling from the roof of a three-storey building. Eighty-year-old Daphne has dislocated her replacement hip while putting on her socks.
In North Yorkshire, first host Carol sprinkles some magic at the fantasy-themed glampsite North Shire.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Justin hosts at his traditional English pub, The Coachman Inn, in Hartford, Cheshire. But a breakfast bombshell and the nearby train station threaten to derail his guests' stay.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Dumfries and Galloway, Henry Cole gives four bar stools a fluorescent makeover, while Simon O'Brien transforms two old sheep shearer seats into an extraordinary bench.
Steve and Janice can't wait to buy a villa with a pool in the pretty Spanish town of Chiclana de la Frontera on the Costa de la Luz. Can Jonnie Irwin find their perfect place for £160k?
Manchester. Johanna dedicates the first night to her favourite food - takeaways - from kebabs to chicken curry. But the main course proves hard for her guests to swallow. (Ep1/5)
Manchester. Lorry-driving film buff Ryan plans a movie-inspired menu, including a 'Prawn Identity' starter, 'Sausage Meat the Fockers' main and 'Chocolate Clockwork Orange' dessert. (Ep2/5)
Manchester. Glamorous 'domestic delegator' Fiona serves a classy but effortless menu. But Linzi takes issue with Fiona's mum chopping the veg and her cousin making the honeycomb. (Ep3/5)
Manchester. After dishing out criticism all week, can launderette owner Linzi impress her guests with her food and ironing competition entertainment, or is she just full of hot air? (Ep4/5)
Manchester. On the final night, Italian Nico plans to show the Brits how it's done. But there are shocks and confrontations, and a game of table football nearly ends in a riot... (Ep5/5)
Alan and Steph hope that their high standards of cleanliness at Grove Barn B&B in Norfolk will lead to sparkling feedback. But Alistair and Scott have a sinking feeling in their bathroom.
Competitive couple Christine and Ian host at Picaroons B&B in Sheringham, Norfolk. But there are plumbing problems and cross words at breakfast.
Stylish Scott and Alistair welcome their rivals to Brightham House in Malborough, Devon, where guests Steph and Alan are dazed by the design and expectations are high for breakfast.
The final visit is to Moran's B&B in Westward Ho! in Devon, owned by ultra-chic retirees John and Jaqueline. But Christine and Ian aren't impressed by the lack of an ensuite.
It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet for the last time. After dramatic discoveries and cutting comments, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
Toyota Supra Mk4: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend restore ex-motorbike-racer Lisa's 1993 Toyota Supra Mk4 in Fife.
Dennis Fire Engine: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend rescue a much-loved 1973 Dennis fire engine, which used to belong to New Romney station in Kent.
Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer house-hunt in London the week after the Brexit vote. Sisters Ruth and Anna pool their funds, while Aba has champagne tastes but lemonade money.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Kirstie Allsopp catches up with Lucia and Matt, who once lived in Royal Wootton Bassett with a yard full of hens, but decided to list, sell up and move to Wales.
...Clive and Kids: The Owens continue to turn an abandoned farmhouse into a 21st-century home. With permission to extend into the barn, there's now even more work to do!
Dad-of-two Tony is rushed to King's after being badly hurt in a road traffic collision. Eric, who's 85, has ongoing stomach problems. Jim, who's 79, has difficulty breathing.
A young man is treated for a severe reaction to a recreational drug, while 65-year-old Christine from Camberwell is brought in to King's by her friends after banging her head.
